content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
October 27,
1992
Ray Saatjian
Ros emakers
9054 Mt. Israel Road
Escondido,
CA
92029
Re:
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No.
1—92—490
Dear Mr.
Saatjian:
This is in response to your letter requesting guidance with
respect to your responsibilities under the “revolving door”
provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the
“Act”) •1
Since you
have requested general information,
we are treating your request
as
one for informal assistance.
2
QUESTION
At the time you terminate your employment with the Del Mar
Fair Board,
the Del
Mar Leasing Commission,
and the Del Mar
Racetrack Authority,
what restrictions will there be
on your post—
employment activity?
CONCLUSION
You may not represent or act as agent or attorney for any
person before the
Del
Mar Fair Board,
the Del Mar Leasing
Commission,
and the
Del
Mar Racetrack Authority for compensation,
for the purpose of influencing administrative action for one year
after leaving your designated position.
1
Government
Code Sections 81000—91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000,
et seq.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114;
Regulation 18329(c) (3).)
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95812 •
(916) 322-5660


Our File No.
1—92—490
Page
2
In addition,
you may not represent any other person before
these agencies in connection with any judicial,
quasi—judicial or
other proceeding in which you participated.
This restriction has
no time limit.
FACTS
You are currently an appointed director for the 22nd District
Agricultural Association
(aka,
the Del Mar Fair Board)
and will
leave that position on January 15,
1993.
You are also a member of the State Racetrack Commission and
the .Racetrack Leasing Commission and will leave that position on
January 15,
1993.
Finally,
you are also a member of the Del Mar Racetrack
Authority and will leave that position on January
15,
1993.
You have asked what restrictions will be placed on your
activity after leaving these entities by the Political Reform Act.
DISCUSSION
1.
Revolving Door Restrictions Under the Ethics Bill
Section 87406 provides that former officers and designated
employees of state agencies are prohibited from representing any
other person for compensation,
before their former public—agency
employer,
or any officer or employee of their former employer,
by
means of formal or informal appearances or oral or written
communications,
for the purpose of influencing:
(1)
The administrative action
3 of the agency;
(2)
Any action or proceeding involving the issuance,
amendment,
awarding,
or revocation of
a permit,
license, grant,
or
contract;
or
(3)
The sale or purchase of goods or property.
This prohibition lasts for one year after leaving office or
employment.
You are a director of the Del Mar Fair Board, the State
Racetrack Commission,
the Racetrack Leasing Commission,
and the
“Administrative action” means the proposal, drafting,
development,
consideration,
amendment,
enactment or defeat by any
state agency of any rule,
regulation or other action in any rate—
making proceeding or any quasi-legislative proceeding,
which shall
include any proceeding governed by Chapter 3.5
(commencing with
Section 11340)
of Part
1 of Division
3 of Title 2.


Our File No.
1—92-490
Page
3
Del Mar Racetrack Authority.
4
Consequently,
for one year after
leaving these offices you may not represent any other person for
compensation before these state agencies.
5
We-have enclosed some
past advice letters discussing activities that are prohibited by
-
this statute for your information.
-
2.
Traditional Revolving Door
-
The Act also imposes a lifetime restriction with respect to
any judicial,
quasi—judicial or other proceeding in which you
participated and in which the State of California is a party or
has a direct and substantial interest.
(Section 87401;
Section
87402.)
These provisions also prohibit aiding,
advising-,
counseling,
consulting or assisting in representing any other
person in these proceedings.
Section 87400(c)
defines “judicial,
quasi-judicial or other
proceeding” as:
[A]ny proceeding,
application,
request for
a
ruling or other determination,
contract,
claim,
controversy,
investigation,
charge,
accusation,
arrest or other particular matter involving a
specific party or parties in any court or state
administrative agency,
including but not limited to
any proceeding governed by Chapter
5
(commencing
with Section 11500)
of Division
3 of Title
2 of the
Government Code.
Thus,
if as an officer of the Del Mar Fair Board,
the State
Racetrack Commission,
the Racetrack Leasing Commission,
or the Del
Mar Racetrack Authority you participated
6
in consideration of a
specific proceeding,
you would be prohibited from representing for
compensation any other person before any court or state
administrative agency or any officer or employee thereof by means
of a formal or informal appearance,
or oral or written
communication or by aiding,
advising,
counseling,
consulting or
assisting any other person in connection with any specific permit,
agreement or contract on which you worked.
We understand from your facts that each of the entities
discussed in your letter are state agencies.
(Se,
Section
82049.)
The “revolving door” provisions only apply to state
officials.
Section 87406 (d) (1)
exempts appearances in a court of law;
- before an administrative law judge;
and,
appearances before the
Worker’s Compensation Appeals Board.
6
You are also deemed to have participated in any proceeding
handled by persons that were under your supervisorial control.
(Brown Advice Letter,
No.
A-91-033.)


Our File No.
1-92-490
Page
4
We have supplemented this letter with
a detailed outline
concerning the “revolving door” provisions of the Act.
You should
refer to the outline with respect to your activities after leaving
your state agency.
If you have any further questions regardin
this matter,
please feel free to contact me at
(916)
322-5901.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
By:
Luisa Menchaca
Counsel,
Legal Division
SH: LM
Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.
Alternatively,
copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of l0 per page.
